treatment_information.treatment_response: Mydriasis was relatively ineffective initially but improved with supplementation.

treatment_timeline.event: Retinal photograph taken showing optic nerve coloboma.
Dense white cataract recorded with no view of the posterior segment; visual acuity less than 1/60.
Patient presented with a change in the appearance of her left pupil; examination revealed subluxation of the lens and total retinal detachment.
Mydriasis with tropicamide (0.5%) and phenylephrine (2.5%) performed.

patient_outcome.final_status: No action taken; advised to report any inflammatory symptoms.
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Patient noticed a change in her appearance in a mirror but had no visual symptoms due to longstanding poor vision.
